title: Songs Without Sense
content:
I. THE PERSONIFIED SENTIMENTAL

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Affectionן¿½s charm no longer gilds
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The idol of the shrine;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But cold Oblivion seeks to fill
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Regretן¿½s ambrosial wine.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Though Friendshipן¿½s offering buried lies
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Neath cold Aversionן¿½s snow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Regard and Faith will ever bloom
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Perpetually below.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I see thee whirl in marble halls,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ In Pleasureן¿½s giddy train;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Remorse is never on that brow,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Nor Sorrowן¿½s mark of pain.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Deceit has marked thee for her own;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Inconstancy the same;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And Ruin wildly sheds its gleam
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Athwart thy path of shame.



II. THE HOMELY PATHETIC

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The dews are heavy on my brow;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ My breath comes hard and low;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Yet, mother dear, grant one request,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Before your boy must go.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh! lift me ere my spirit sinks,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And ere my senses fail,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Place me once more, O mother dear,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Astride the old fence-rail.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The old fence-rail, the old fence-rail!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ How oft these youthful legs,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With Aliceן¿½ and Ben Boltן¿½s, were hung
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Across those wooden pegs!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Twas there the nauseating smoke
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of my first pipe arose:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ O mother dear, these agonies
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Are far less keen than those.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I know where lies the hazel dell,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Where simple Nellie sleeps;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I know the cot of Nettie Moore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And where the willow weeps.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ I know the brookside and the mill,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ But all their pathos fails
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Beside the days when once I sat
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Astride the old fence-rails.



III. SWISS AIR

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Iן¿½m a gay tra, la, la,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With my fal, lal, la, la,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And my bright
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And my light
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Tra, la, le. [Repeat.]

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then laugh, ha, ha, ha,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And ring, ting, ling, ling,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And sing fal, la, la,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ La, la, le. [Repeat.]
meaning:
This evocative piece by Bret Harte (Francis) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Bret Harte (Francis)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
